title: Query to check activities that were assigned to me on a board and that left an impression on me
excerpt: Just to give you some context: every week, I need to create a list of all the reports that were requested from me in the previous week. So I thought of creating a Chrome extension where I can add the API key and my Monda…

posts.author: SantanaWebDev (Isaias Santana dos Santos)
posts.cookedHtml:
Just to give you some context: every week, I need to create a list of all the reports that were requested from me in the previous week. So I thought of creating a Chrome extension where I can add the API key and my Monday user ID so that it is “saved” in the browser. Once this is done, just add the board ID and click to generate a file (Excel, PDF or Word), with all the requests that were assigned to me on the board, or those that just mentioned me.
I have no knowledge of GraphQl and I am having difficulty creating the query. Could you tell me how to do this?

posts.author: Billchenau (Bill)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @SantanaWebDev

Bill here from monday.com

You’ll need to use a combination of queries to enable this.

  1. items_page_by_column_values: You can use the board ID as an input argument in this query as well as the column ID and values you are looking to filter for to return a list of items assigned to you.
  2. Updates: You may also need to query for the updates contained in these items using this query.

If you needed any additional support on this would you be able to please fill this form adding as much information as possible to it (such as account ID, board IDs, item IDs, timestamps, etc.) so that our team can continue to support you on this?

I hope this helps.

Cheers,
Bill

posts.author: SantanaWebDev (Isaias Santana dos Santos)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Bill,

Thank you for your feedback. I’m responding now because a month ago our company returned 100% in person and the adaptation is slow.

But I hope that in a few days I will continue with this project, and if I have any questions, I will ask again, ok?

Thank you so much for your support, it means a lot to me!

Thanks,
